 {"id":"br-1bm.6.3","title":"HTTP Logging Parity: OTEL config no-op","description":"## Objective
Ensure OTEL config variables are accepted but behave as no‑op (legacy parity).

## Scope
- Env vars: `HTTP_OTEL_ENABLED`, `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`.
- When enabled, **do not** crash if OTEL exporter unavailable; effectively no‑op.
- Preserve request logging behavior independent of OTEL settings.

## Tests
- Unit tests verifying no errors when OTEL vars are set.
- Integration tests with OTEL enabled and missing exporter (should still serve requests).

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store OTEL config logs under `tests/artifacts/http/logging/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:26.087167604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:31:24.229624529Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.6.3","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:26.087167604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.6.3","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:39.672384539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.6.4","title":"HTTP Logging Parity: unit + integration + E2E tests","description":"Comprehensive tests for HTTP logging parity (request logs + structlog renderers + ExpectedErrorFilter), with detailed artifacts.

Test layers:
- Unit tests:
  - formatting normalization (TTY vs non-TTY)
  - conditional enablement: HTTP_REQUEST_LOG_ENABLED only
  - LOG_JSON_ENABLED toggles JSONRenderer vs KeyValueRenderer (key_order=[event,path,status])
  - field derivation (client_ip from request.client.host, latency int ms)
  - ExpectedErrorFilter classifier:
    - expected patterns detected and downgraded
    - tracebacks suppressed for expected errors
    - cause-chain inspection behavior
- Integration tests:
  - server with logging enabled emits logs
  - server with logging disabled emits none
  - LOG_JSON_ENABLED branch executed on app build (no crash)
  - OTEL flags enabled: no-op parity (no spans, no behavior change)
- E2E (part of scripts/e2e_http.sh, br-2ei.9.6):
  - launch server with logging on
  - issue representative requests (2xx/4xx/5xx)
  - capture server stderr/stdout logs + HTTP transcripts
  - store artifacts under tests/artifacts/http/<timestamp>/

Legacy test references:
- test_logging_and_redis_fallback.py::test_log_json_enabled_path
- test_server.py::test_rich_logger_does_not_throw
- test_expected_error_filter.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit + integration tests cover the enable/disable matrix and TTY/non-TTY output.
- Snapshot/golden log assertions are driven by the spec outputs from br-1bm.6.1.
- ExpectedErrorFilter behavior matches legacy tests and pattern list.
- E2E artifacts include clear expected-vs-actual field diffs and captured logs.
- Tests run deterministically (fixed timestamps/latency fakes where required).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:30.521330089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:47:58.051121116Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.6.4","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:30.521330089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.6.4","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:43.034853482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.6.4","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:46.092367952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.6.4","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:08:56.319100804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.7","title":"CORS Parity Validation","description":"Objective:
Validate and complete CORS behavior parity with legacy Python: origins, allow_methods, allow_headers, allow_credentials, and OPTIONS responses. Ensure behavior matches when lists are empty or "*".

Legacy Reference Behavior:
- If CORS enabled, allow_origins defaults to ["*"] when empty
- allow_methods default ["*"]
- allow_headers default ["*"]
- Credentials flag respected

Implementation Plan:
- Ensure CORS config parsing matches legacy defaults.
- Verify OPTIONS responses include appropriate headers.
- Ensure origin matching allows all when list is empty.

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
- Unit tests for allowlist matching and default behavior.
- Integration tests for OPTIONS + GET/POST with Origin set.
- E2E coverage via `br-2ei.9.6` (HTTP parity suite) with header transcripts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CORS headers match legacy defaults and behavior across:
  - empty lists
  - explicit "*"
  - allow_credentials on/off
- Preflight (OPTIONS) behavior matches legacy (status + headers).
- E2E artifacts include explicit header diffs on mismatch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:03.472355999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:23:06.874227229Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:23:06.874209255Z","close_reason":"CORS parity: env defaults + wildcard handling + header override + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.7","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:03.472355999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:08:56.401992322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-1bm.8","title":"Bearer Auth Parity","description":"Objective:
Ensure Bearer token auth middleware behavior matches legacy Python, including localhost bypass logic and forwarded-header safeguards.

Legacy Reference Behavior:
- If bearer token configured, require Authorization: Bearer <token>
- If allow_localhost_unauthenticated and client is localhost (no forwarded headers), bypass Authorization
- OPTIONS and /health/* always allowed
- Constant-time comparison for token equality

Implementation Plan:
- Verify Rust middleware path covers OPTIONS + /health/* bypass.
- Enforce constant-time token compare.
- Use peer_addr + forwarded headers for localhost detection (`br-1bm.3`).

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
- Unit tests for token comparison + bypass conditions.
- Integration tests for /health endpoints, OPTIONS, and standard POSTs.
- E2E coverage via `br-2ei.9.6` (HTTP parity suite) including forwarded-header negative cases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Responses and bypass behavior match legacy exactly (status + JSON body).
- Constant-time compare is used (no early-return string compare).
- E2E artifacts include explicit allow/deny traces and header diffs on mismatch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:12.124733018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:40:29.656247547Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:12.124733018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:23.276913856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:08:56.484645140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1bm.9","title":"Health + Well-Known Endpoints Parity Tests","description":"Objective:
Verify health and well-known endpoints match legacy behavior and remain stable. Ensure status codes and JSON payloads are identical.

Endpoints:
- GET /health/liveness -> {"status":"alive"}
- GET /health/readiness -> {"status":"ready"} OR 503 {"detail":"..."}
- GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server -> {"mcp_oauth": false}
- GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/mcp -> {"mcp_oauth": false}

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
- Unit tests for readiness error formatting.
- Integration tests for each endpoint and method mismatch (405).
- E2E coverage via `br-2ei.9.6` (HTTP parity suite): hit each endpoint, log status/body, fail on mismatch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON payloads and status codes identical to legacy for all endpoints.
- Method mismatch behavior matches legacy (e.g., 405).
- E2E artifacts include per-endpoint transcript and diffs on mismatch.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:18.747115290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:40:36.900420537Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.9","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:18.747115290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.9","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:08:56.567818709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1e5h","title":"DB concurrency parity: backoff + circuit breaker","description":"## Objective
Implement database lock contention handling and circuit breaker per legacy concurrency model.

## Scope
- Exponential backoff with jitter on SQLITE_BUSY/locked errors.
- Circuit breaker: after 5 consecutive failures, open for 30s (fail fast with `DATABASE_POOL_EXHAUSTED`/`RESOURCE_BUSY`).
- Connection pooling defaults: base 3 + overflow 4, recycle 30min, busy timeout 5s.

## Tests
- Unit tests simulating lock contention and backoff sequence.
- Integration tests for circuit breaker open/close behavior.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store backoff/circuit logs under `tests/artifacts/db/concurrency/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Backoff and circuit breaker match legacy thresholds and timings.
2. Errors are mapped to legacy error codes.
3. Tests are deterministic and do not rely on real contention.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:50.675607847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:21:45.705741026Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:21:45.705722471Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1e5h","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:55.369503695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Integration tests on seeded snapshot DB with attachments and secrets.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Preset behavior matches legacy exactly (counts + outputs).
2. Secret patterns are redacted without altering non‑secrets.
3. Attachments scrubbed per key list with correct counts.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:20.358844765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:40:16.312540941Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1nh","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:23.418674217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1t0e","title":"Resources: file_reservations resource should be bounded (avoid glob explosion)","description":"## Problem
`resource://file_reservations/{slug}` currently expands glob patterns via filesystem enumeration and may run `git log` over many pathspecs. On large projects with broad patterns (e.g. `src/**`), this can take >60s and effectively wedges the MCP server (subsequent tool/resource calls time out).

## Objective
Make `resource://file_reservations/{slug}` reliably fast and non-blocking for large repos while keeping legacy output shape.

## Scope
- Avoid enumerating all filesystem matches for glob patterns.
- Compute git activity without expanding to thousands of explicit paths (use git pathspec/glob or prefix-based strategy).
- Bound work per pattern (time/size caps) and fail open by returning `null` activity timestamps when exceeding caps.
- Ensure cleanup logic (TTL expiry + stale release) cannot monopolize the server request thread.

## Tests
- Unit tests covering glob-heavy patterns (`src/**`, `crates/**`, `**/*.rs`) ensure the code path does not enumerate full repo trees (use a temp dir with many files to assert completes quickly).
- Regression test: resource handler completes within a small time budget under a synthetic repo with many files and a broad glob reservation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `resource://file_reservations/...` returns within a bounded time on large repos (no tool server wedge).
2. Output shape remains compatible (fields present, timestamps may be null when capped).
3. Existing conformance tests still pass; add targeted tests for the new caps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:05.771837055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745259737Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745241343Z","close_reason":"Avoid glob explosion in resource://file_reservations: stop filesystem glob expansion; use git pathspec glob magic; add regression test; ran fmt+clippy+test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1t0e","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:34.586726901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

1. All steps are implemented per spec with legacy parity.
2. Deterministic bundles (stable hashes) are enforced in tests.
3. E2E share/export suite passes with detailed artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"PearlOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:14:52.252531998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:40:08.440265051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1uf","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:14:56.877509340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-27m","title":"Share: search indexes + materialized views + perf indexes","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 4–6: FTS5 search indexes, materialized views, and performance indexes for static viewer.

## Scope
- **build_search_indexes**: create `fts_messages` virtual table; populate with subject/body/importance/project/thread_key/created_ts; optimize.
- **build_materialized_views**: create `message_overview_mv`, `attachments_by_message_mv`, `fts_search_overview_mv` (if FTS enabled).
- **create_performance_indexes**: add lowercase columns and covering indexes for fast viewer filtering.

## Tests
- Unit tests for FTS presence/absence path.
- Integration tests verifying view row counts and index existence.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store schema dumps under `tests/artifacts/share/indexes/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. FTS and MV tables match legacy schemas and row counts.
2. Performance indexes created without errors, idempotent.
3. Viewer search works against generated bundle DB.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:32.931924389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:00:45.523981969Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:00:45.523958655Z","close_reason":"Validated share index/view parity; ensured message_recipients table exists","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-27m","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:36.800683710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28u","title":"Share: chunk database (4MiB chunks)","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Step 9: database chunking for large bundles.

## Scope
- Default threshold: 20MiB; chunk size 4MiB.
- Split DB file into sequential chunks with deterministic naming/order.
- Update manifest to reference chunks and original size.

## Tests
- Integration test with synthetic DB large enough to trigger chunking.
- Verify chunk count, sizes, and reassembly hash match original.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store chunk metadata under `tests/artifacts/share/chunking/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Chunking triggers only above threshold and produces correct chunk sizes.
2. Reassembly yields byte‑identical DB.
3. Manifest updated with chunk metadata.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:09.803257199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:49:54.658358572Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:49:54.658340589Z","close_reason":"Chunking already implemented in bundle.rs with tests; verified chunk_* tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28u","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:14.635619585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2b9","title":"Share: finalize snapshot for export (VACUUM/page size/journal)","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Step 7: finalize snapshot for export.

## Scope
- Set `journal_mode=DELETE` and `page_size=1024`.
- Run `VACUUM` and `ANALYZE` to compact snapshot.
- Ensure resulting DB is read‑only friendly and minimal.

## Tests
- Integration test verifying PRAGMA settings and DB integrity.
- Ensure file size shrinks relative to pre‑finalize snapshot.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store PRAGMA outputs and file size stats under `tests/artifacts/share/finalize/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"br-36w","title":"Config parity: .env loading + env var coverage","description":"## Objective
Match legacy configuration behavior, including `.env` loading and full env var coverage.

## Scope
- `.env` loading behavior (python‑decouple parity):
  - if .env missing → empty repository fallback; env vars only.
  - canonical env var set only if missing when alias present (LLM bridge).
- Ensure all env vars from spec are supported:
  - WORKTREES_ENABLED + GIT_IDENTITY_ENABLED gate
  - ALLOW_ABSOLUTE_ATTACHMENT_PATHS
  - TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED, LOG_RICH_ENABLED
  - NOTIFICATIONS_* defaults
  - HTTP_* (CORS/JWT/RBAC/rate limit)
  - LLM_* defaults
- CSV parsing for list settings (roles, tools, clusters).
- Match default values exactly.

## Tests
- Unit tests for .env presence/absence handling.
- Table‑driven env parsing tests for bools/ints/CSV lists.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store parsed config snapshots under `tests/artifacts/config/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Rust config matches legacy defaults and parsing semantics.
2. `.env` loading behavior matches python‑decouple (graceful missing file).
3. All env variables from spec are supported and tested.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:09.094825172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:50.832107234Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:50.832088128Z","close_reason":"Implemented .env loading + env var coverage + tests; checks blocked by sqlmodel-sqlite duplicate defs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-36w","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:12.895192349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-3np","title":"CLI: share export/update parity (interactive + defaults)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share export` and `share update` commands with legacy behavior (including `--interactive`).

## Scope
- Flags: `--output/-o`, `--interactive/-i`, `--project/-p` (repeat), thresholds, scrub preset, chunk settings, `--dry-run`, `--zip`, `--signing-key`, `--signing-public-out`, `--age-recipient` (repeat).
- Defaults: inline=64KiB, detach=25MiB, chunk threshold=20MiB, chunk size=4MiB; scrub preset `standard`.
- Validation: invalid preset exits 1; enforce chunk size >=1024; auto‑adjust detach threshold if <= inline.
- `share export`:
  - `--dry-run` creates temp output + prints summary only.
  - `--interactive` wizard prompts and overrides flags.
- `share update`:
  - loads config from existing `manifest.json` and stored export config.
  - default `--zip` is **false** (contrast export default true).

## Tests
- Unit tests for flag parsing + defaults.
- Integration tests with temp bundle export/update; verify manifest config updates.
- Golden output for `--dry-run` summary.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store CLI outputs under `tests/artifacts/cli/share/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share export/update match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.
2. interactive mode overrides flags and produces identical outputs to legacy.
3. dry-run produces no bundle artifacts but prints expected summary.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:51.526681473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:33.897026002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3np","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:33.896970708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3np","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:56.127446696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
